title: March 2023 thoughts — ChatGPT, becoming obsolete, generation-defining moment, Bill Gates's PC era
html:
March 2023 thoughts — ChatGPT, becoming obsolete, generation-defining moment, Bill Gates's PC era

Source: 2023-03-24 Google Doc. The single most important AI-thesis note in the pre-Rankett corpus — captures Yahir's realization in March 2023 that AI/ChatGPT is the generation-defining moment, and his anxiety about being late.

TL;DR

  • "This is all bullshit to what is happening with AI. When I was a freshman in highschool, I called out that I knew that I needed to get involved with AI."
  • "ChatGPT is the ultimate things... I feel like I am becoming obsolete with the fucking technology. It feels like everything that I can do will only be done better by the AI."
  • The wipeout-and-niche thesis (proto-Rankett): "I feel like I am very capable of finding a niche within all of this. Almost like creating a little safe haven pocket where I am safe from the wipeout, mainly because I am leveraging the wipeout."
  • Macro hot-takes (March 2023):
    • Google has missed the mark with AI — "BARD is a fail and the innovators dilemma has smacked them in the face."
    • US banking on the brink of chaos and collapse (the SVB era).
    • Russia + China same-page-but-acting-alone, want to take down US.
  • Generation-thinking framework:
    • PC era (80s): Bill Gates, Steve Jobs, Larry Ellison.
    • Dotcom (90s): Elon Musk, Mark Cuban, Larry Page.
    • Social Media (00s-10s): Mark Zuckerberg, Jack Dorsey, Chamath Palihapitiya.
    • "How will I be apart of the group in my generation that stands out?"
  • The validation thread: started with not getting validation from OSIG (Investment Group), echoed Chamath's point about validation; then Mari the cross-country attractive runner in pickleball PAC said "validation comes from within." Yahir grappling with self-validation as an 21-year-old.
  • Anti-Stanford-PhD anxiety: "all of these motherfuckers are unquestionably smart, whip smart. PHD at Stanford type of smart." Then the David-Morris-vs-Kai memory: someone hyped up to be unbeatable in football turned out to be ordinary. "In the end we are all relatively similar."
  • Steve Jobs frame: "He mentioned that everything that we see around us was made by people that are no smarter than you or I. Just ordinary people that decided saw a problem in the world and decided to pursue it."

Key quote

"I'm anxious, nervous, weirdly uncertain about what the next years will bring. In many facets, love, legacy, life experience, it feels like an absolute whirlwind of life will be thrown our way."

Pattern observed

This is the founding document of Yahir's AI thesis — written 4 months after ChatGPT's Nov 2022 launch, ~2.5 years before Rankett crystallizes. Three things stick:

  1. The "leveraging the wipeout" framing is Rankett's eventual positioning verbatim — Yahir already saw the structural opportunity.
  2. The generation-defining-moment framing (PC, Dotcom, Social, AI) is the macro-thesis backbone that informs every later business decision.
  3. The anti-credentialism Jobs framing (you're no dumber than Stanford PhDs) is the psychological permission that Yahir grants himself to compete.

The Mari-validation insight ("validation comes from within") becomes a recurring touchstone — appears in 2024 09 12 never forget do whatever as "don't seek revenge / give it your true effort and you can go to bed resting well."

The note also signals Yahir's macro-news appetite in 2023 — he was tracking SVB / Russia-China geopolitics / Google AI strategy in real time. This appetite informs the Masayoshi Son / Chamath / Mark Cuban mentor-tracking pattern.

Open questions

  • Mari (the runner) — first mention. Same Mari elsewhere?
  • "David Morris" middle-school football — embedded memory used as data point. Worth its own entity? (Probably no — single-mention.)
  • The "freshman in high school I called out AI" claim — is there a corresponding Apple Note from 2017-18? (Currently not visible in raw/.)
